question:
Will the price of Bitcoin be above $112,000 on November 11?

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

evidences.title: Coinbase Institutional x Glassnode Insight: Q4 2025 Crypto Outlook | Mohammad Javad Nazari posted on the topic | LinkedIn
evidences.content: October 2025 proved a volatile period for the digital asset ecosystem, characterized by sharp peaks, steep corrections, and a gradual rebound fueled by institutional interest and regulatory momentum. Bitcoin touched record highs before a rapid retreat, while Ethereum also displayed upward pressure through enhanced DeFi engagement before a subsequent downward push. Altcoins showed inconsistent patterns, with select platforms riding ETF optimism and network upgrades amid macroeconomic factors like Federal Reserve actions and trade frictions. Stablecoins and tokenized assets showed underlying resilience, though traders grappled with liquidation waves and shifting global narratives. This overview examines pivotal trends, coin performances, and catalysts steering the industry. Blake Namerow [...] Key Highlights from the Coinbase Institutional x Glassnode Insight – Q4 2025 📊 Despite the leverage flush on October 10, the report anticipates continued support from robust liquidity, a constructive macro backdrop, and encouraging regulatory developments. Analysts believe the crypto bull market still has room to run, and that fears of a sharp economic slowdown appear overstated. 💡 Investor...

evidences.title: Coinbase x Glassnode Report: Crypto Market Outlook for Q4 2025
evidences.content: Q4 2025 - Charting the Crypto Landscape Official Link: The latest Coinbase x Glassnode “Charting Crypto” report does a great job of capturing where the market really stands heading into Q4 2025. Despite all the noise, the tone feels cautiously optimistic. Liquidity remains healthy, and with upcoming regulatory clarity, confidence is slowly returning. What stood out to me: - 67% of institutional investors are still bullish on Bitcoin, conviction hasn’t faded. - Digital Asset Treasuries (#DATs) are quietly becoming a force, holding large chunks of #BTC and #ETH, reshaping market liquidity. - Innovation continues around automated yield and structured products, showing that builders never stopped building. And on the horizon, new policies like the #GENIUS and #CLARITY Acts could finally [...] Reflecting on the recent crypto flash crash, where Bitcoin dropped 10-13% amid trade tensions and over $500 billion vanished from the market, I’ve been pondering a subtle yet profound vulnerability: the depegging of Ethena’s USDe to $0.65 on Binance. What strikes me is how synthetic, crypto collateralised stablecoins like USDe differ from fiat backed ones, amplifying liquidity risks during dele...

evidences.title: Coinbase–Glassnode survey: Bitcoin outlook is 'cautiously optimistic'
evidences.content: $0.0000514 -7.74625 Asteroid Shiba price # Coinbase–Glassnode survey: Bitcoin outlook is ‘cautiously optimistic’ Alexey Borovets By Alexey Borovets Jayson Derrick Edited by Jayson Derrick Follow-up A new study by Coinbase and Glassnode indicates most investors are optimistic about Bitcoin in the next 3-6 months. Share A report released by Coinbase Institutional and Glassnode on Oct. 20, 2025, reveals that most investors believe the Bitcoin bull market will continue over the next 3–6 months. Researchers surveyed institutional investors and produced a crypto market outlook based on their responses. The report’s subtitle, “Navigating Uncertainty,” resonates with the recent nosedive following a new Bitcoin all-time high. Summary [...] The report begins with a foreword by David Duong, Coinbase Institutional’s head of research, who expects favorable macroeconomic, regulatory, and policy conditions. He believes digital asset treasury companies will continue to amplify crypto demand; new rate cuts by year-end could help mobilize $7 trillion in idle funds. Duong also outlines several challenges, including the government shutdown, which limits access to key economic data, and the uncertai...

evidences.title: Coinbase Institutional Highlights Three Catalysts That Could Lift Crypto in Q4 2025
evidences.content: Coinbase Institutional Highlights Three Catalysts That Could Lift Crypto in Q4 2025 · CoinDesk Siamak Masnavi 2 min read COIN +2.05% BTC-USD +2.84% Crypto's year-end setup still skews positive, according to Coinbase Institutional's fourth-quarter 2025 "Charting Crypto: Navigating Uncertainty" report produced in collaboration with Glassnode. The team characterizes its stance as cautious but biased higher after the Oct. 10 shakeout. Coinbase links the slide to heavy leverage meeting thin order books, then worsening when some exchanges' auto-deleveraging capped market-maker shorts and drained liquidity. The firm says prices stabilized into the weekend, but the tape stayed tentative as macro jitters resurfaced. Liquidity and macro sit at the center of Coinbase's outlook. [...] The report highlights stablecoin supply and monthly volumes at or near record levels — evidence, in Coinbase's telling, that more payments and transfers are moving on-chain. It also notes that U.S. spot ETF infrastructure for bitcoin and ether continues to deepen, improving access for traditional allocators and strengthening market depth. These developments, Coinbase argues, are less about headlines and more a...

evidences.content: 💡 Download Charting Crypto Q3 2025 by Coinbase x Glassnode Highlights from this edition: Bitcoin leads the market as dominance reaches 64%, fueled by ETF inflows and renewed institutional accumulation. Stablecoins solidify their role in the financial stack, with total supply surpassing $230B and monthly volumes exceeding $4T. Ethereum shows signs of rotation, as investors reposition following Q1’s capitulation and Q2’s strong recovery. With analysis from both Coinbase and Glassnode analyst teams, this quarterly report remains a core resource for professional investors navigating crypto’s latest developments. ### Key Insights from Q3 2025 [...] ### Key Insights from Q3 2025 1. evidences.title: May 2025: U.S. Fiscal Risks Driving Bitcoin Demand | Grayscale
evidences.content: The U.S.-listed spot ETPs have arguably been the single most important source of new demand for Bitcoin since they launched. These products continued to see elevated net inflows during May, totaling $5.2bn(#_ftn5). In the coming months, the volume of Bitcoin purchases from the spot Bitcoin ETPs could potentially be matched or exceeded by purchases from “Bitcoin treasury” corporations — i.e., public companies purchasing Bitcoin for their balance sheet. Strategy (formerly MicroStrategy), which pioneered corporate Bitcoin investing, purchased an additional ~27k Bitcoin (~$2.8bn) in May.(#_ftn6) Strategy’s market cap is well in excess of the value of the Bitcoin on its balance sheet, indicating excess demand for Bitcoin exposure in the form of an equity instrument (Exhibit 3). [...] Exhibit 3: Strategy valued at premium to its Bitcoin holdings

evidences.title: MicroStrategy Stock and Bitcoin 2025: Dive into Bitcoin's Impact
evidences.content: ### Insights from Industry Experts Analysts remain divided on MicroStrategy’s prospects. Some highlights include unmatched innovation and substantial Bitcoin holdings, predicting massive future returns if Bitcoin adoption increases. Others warn of risks associated with Bitcoin’s volatility and regulatory uncertainties. Bitcoin’s price surged past $100,000 in 2024, driven by the introduction of Bitcoin ETFs and political factors, with predictions of reaching $200,000 by late 2025. Companies like MicroStrategy, holding substantial Bitcoin, could see extreme returns. However, changes in regulations and market fluctuations are crucial factors that may impact these predictions. ## Investing in MicroStrategy [...] MicroStrategy has made headlines with its bold investment strategy in Bitcoin. Consequently, investors are wondering about MicroStrategy’s stock predictions for 2025. Predictions for MicroStrategy stock in 2025 show high variability. Bitcoin holdings heavily influence them. MarketBeat suggests a price range from $409 to $650. The average is $536.90. Stock Analysis forecasts an average target price of $382.10, with a potential reach of up to $650. Analysts are generally optim...

evidences.title: The Rise of Digital Asset Treasury Companies (DATCOs) - Crypto Treasury Strategy | Galaxy
evidences.content: MicroStrategy’s move boosted the company’s market capitalization and created a new narrative around BTC as a corporate treasury that others would follow. Saylor’s playbook (issuing debt to acquire more BTC, launching ATM equity programs, and bull-posting BTC publicly) established a repeatable model. For years, MicroStrategy stood alone, but it laid the foundation for future entrants. ### 2023-2025 From 2023 to 2025, several major catalysts fueled the rise of DATCOs from an isolated phenomenon to a formal narrative: A 2023 FASB accounting update allowed fair-value treatment of digital assets, enabling public companies to mark their crypto holdings to market. The SEC’s approval of U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs in 2024 validated bitcoin’s place in mainstream financial infrastructure.
evidences.query: BlackRock IBIT Fidelity FBTC spot Bitcoin ETF inflows MicroStrategy Treasury holdings Bitcoin demand impact 2025

evidences.content: d = d.replace(tzinfo=pytz.utc) # return the difference in time return int((d - epoch).total_seconds() 1000.0) def interval_to_milliseconds(interval): """Convert a Binance interval string to milliseconds :param interval: Binance interval string 1m, 3m, 5m, 15m, 30m, 1h, 2h, 4h, 6h, 8h, 12h, 1d, 3d, 1w :type interval: str :return: None if unit not one of m, h, d or w None if string not in correct format int value of interval in milliseconds """ ms = None seconds_per_unit = { "m": 60, "h": 60 60, "d": 24 60 60, "w": 7 24 60 60 } unit = interval[-1] if unit in seconds_per_unit: try: ms = int(interval[:-1]) seconds_per_unit[unit] 1000 except ValueError: pass return ms def GetUpdateData(kline): Time = time.strftime("%a, %d %b %Y %H:%M:%S", time.gmtime(kline/1000)) Open = kline [...] Open = kline High = kline Low = kline Close = kline Volume = kline Close_time = time.strftime("%a, %d %b %Y %H:%M:%S", time.localtime(kline/1000)) Quote_asset_volume = kline Number_of_trades = kline Taker_buy_base_asset_volume = kline Taker_buy_quote_asset_volume = kline return Time,Open,High,Low,Close,Volume,Close_time,Quote_asset_volume,Number_of_trades,Taker_buy_base_asset_volume,Taker_buy_quote_asset_v...

evidences.title: Bitcoin Price Drawdown from ATH Chart
evidences.content: ### Historical Drawdowns and Current Cycle Comparisons In previous Bitcoin market cycles, the percentage drawdown has varied significantly: Cycle 1 (2010 - 2013) - Peak: December 2013 - Drawdown: 93% from ATH Cycle 2 (2014 to 2017) • Peak: December 2017 • Drawdown: ~86-87% from ATH Cycle 3 (2018 to 2021) • Peak: November 2021 • Drawdown: ~75-77% from ATH ### Spikes and Cooldown Phases: A Pattern to Watch One of the most intriguing aspects of Bitcoin’s historical drawdowns is the tendency for spikes in price recovery during the bear market before cooling off again. For instance: • In 2015, Bitcoin experienced a spike that saw the drawdown reduce to just 32% below its ATH before cooling off for several months. [...] Market Sentiment and Timing: Bitcoin tends to follow similar behavioral patterns across cycles, with sharp sell-offs followed by long accumulation periods. Understanding these phases helps investors identify periods of heightened risk and opportunity. Cross-Asset Comparisons: By examining Bitcoin’s drawdown alongside other assets like Ethereum, gold, and traditional market indices, investors can get a clearer picture of Bitcoin’s relative strength, aiding in diversifie...

evidences.title: Seasonality, the bitcoin halving, US elections and the FED ...
evidences.content: In both 2016 and 2020, bitcoin experienced periods of sideways trading or even dipped below its halving price for a period after each event. Approximately 160 days following each halving, the price in all instances had risen above the halving level, although this year’s price difference is notably smaller than in the previous two halvings. In 2016 and 2020, substantial price rallies occurred later, particularly in the year following the halving—specifically in 2017 and 2021. To illustrate, after the second halving, bitcoin surged from around $770 (approximately 160 days post-halving) to over $19’000 by December 2017. Likewise, following the third halving, bitcoin climbed from $11’000 (160 days post-halving) to $69’000 by November 2021. [...] ##### The bitcoin halving On April 19, 2024, the fourth bitcoin halving took place. We have previously published several articles on this topic, which you can revisit for more context. In this section, we will compare bitcoin's price performance following previous halvings with its current performance in 2024, after the fourth halving. As mentioned earlier, seasonality plays a minor role in price movements; significant events and news—such a...

evidences.title: Binance Historical Tick Data (1 second) for a specific date range and symbol - Stack Overflow
evidences.content: binance Share Improve this question edited Aug 19, 2023 at 17:02 asked Aug 19, 2023 at 6:54 Optimus Prime's user avatar Optimus Prime 6344 silver badges1414 bronze badges 0 Add a comment | ## 1 Answer 1 Reset to default 3 +50 Binance provides some of its historical trading data on the `binance.vision` domain. Specifically, the 1 second klines for BTCUSDT pair can be found here: Here's a sample bash script that downloads and unpacks the raw data for the whole month: [...] ``` 1688169600000,30471.99000000,30472.00000000,30471.99000000,30472.00000000,0.54114000,1688169600999,16489.61790060,31,0.52320000,15942.95040000,0 ``` enables you to calculate this: From millisecond timestamp `1688169600000` (July 1st, 2023 0:00:00 UTC) To millisecond timestamp `1688169600999` (July 1st, 2023 0:00:00.999 UTC) Full candle + Open price `30471.99` USDT for 1 BTC + Close price `30472.00` USDT for 1 BTC + Close is higher than open => candle is green Knots + Lowest price `30471.99` USDT for 1 BTC - Same as open price of green candle => no knot below + Highest price `30472.00` USDT for 1 BTC - Same as close price of green candle => no knot above Share Improve this answer answered Aug 21, 2023 at 21:4...
